stocks.recent_news: Q4/FY26 results released Apr 21, 2026: PAT up 18% YoY to Rs 2,291 cr; GNPA dropped to 0.93% (below 1% threshold); NIM improved 6bps QoQ to 3.69%. Final dividend Rs 8/share declared. Branch network expanded to 393 (37 added in FY26). Stock jumped 8.92% on results day; trading near Rs 1,047 (Apr 27). Management guides 20% loan growth but flags margin narrowing.
stocks.sector_context: Indian housing finance sector in expansion phase: home loan market USD 430.7bn in 2026, projected 13.44% CAGR through 2031. NBFCs growing 15-17% in FY26; affordable housing (<Rs 25L) growing 22-23%. RBI held repo at 5.25% in April 2026 with neutral stance after delivering 125bps cumulative cuts in FY25. Larger well-capitalized HFCs accelerating; smaller players face funding/compliance pressures.
stocks.peer_comparison: Asset quality (GNPA 0.93%) leads sector vs LIC Housing/Bajaj Housing. LIC Housing guiding 10-13% loan growth; Bajaj Housing AUM Rs 1.23L cr. PNBHOUSING's 20% growth target is aggressive but margin narrowing concern is sector-wide.
red_flags: Management acknowledges margin compression risk going into FY27
Aggressive 20% loan growth target may stress underwriting if economy weakens
Stock has run up ~15%+ post-results - momentum-chasing risk at current levels
catalysts: Q1 FY27 GNPA sub-1% sustainability test
Branch expansion benefits compounding under new RBI authorisation rules

stocks.recent_news: ETF trading at Rs 121.58 (Apr 29). MCX Gold reclaimed Rs 1.50 lakh/10g on Apr 30 (intraday low Rs 1,49,069); spot gold above USD 4,710/oz. AUM Rs 54,127 cr (largest gold ETF in India); expense ratio 0.80%. 52-wk range Rs 76.36-148.14 (currently mid-range, ~18% below high). Silver also rallying.
stocks.sector_context: Gold consolidating near elevated levels. Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75% for third consecutive meeting in April with notable hawkish dissent (8 of 12 voted for pause - highest dissent since Oct 1992). Medium-term gold outlook positive on inflation, central bank buying, and geopolitical risks (Middle East 9-week conflict, Strait of Hormuz effective closure, US-Iran talks). JPMorgan projects USD 5,000/oz by Q4 2026. DXY at 98-99 caps near-term upside.
stocks.peer_comparison: GOLDBEES tracks domestic gold prices closely; performance in line with other gold ETFs (NIPPONGOLD, AXISGOLD, HDFCMFGETF). Liquidity and AUM scale advantage vs peers. Expense ratio 0.80% slightly higher than newer ETF entrants.
red_flags: Currently in consolidation phase, ~18% below 52-wk high suggests momentum cooling
Hawkish Fed dissent could pressure short-term prices if rate cuts delayed
Dollar strength (DXY ~98-99) historically caps gold upside
catalysts: Next FOMC meeting and rate path clarity
Indian wedding/Akshaya Tritiya physical demand seasonality

stocks.recent_news: MAJOR: Announced acquisition of US-based Organon for USD 11.75bn on Apr 27 - largest deal in Indian pharma history. Shares jumped 7% on news. Acquisition lifts combined revenue to ~USD 12.4bn (top 25 global pharma); Organon brings women's health and biosimilars portfolio (70+ products in 140 countries) at USD 14.00/share all-cash. Incorporated wholly-owned subsidiary Sun Pharma America Inc on Apr 23. Trading near Rs 1,747 (Apr 28). Q3 FY26 PAT up 16.03% to Rs 3,368.81 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Pharma a defensive bet in 2026: Nifty Pharma down only 3% YTD vs Nifty 50 down 12%. Domestic pharma market grew 12.4% YoY in Feb 2026 (third consecutive double-digit month). Trump issued Apr 2 Proclamation imposing 100% tariffs on patented pharma imports under Section 232; CRITICAL: Indian generics and biosimilars EXEMPT (90%+ of US exports protected); tariffs take effect Jul 31/Sep 29, 2026. One-year review clause is tail risk for generics post-2027. USFDA OAI rate dropped to 8% (from 12% in 2015) - compliance improving.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest Indian pharma by market cap; Organon deal differentiates Sun materially from Dr Reddy's, Cipla, Torrent. Tariff exemption on generics benefits all Indian pharma but Sun's diversification (specialty + generics) reduces US generic dependency. 57 approved NDAs, 14 pending - among industry leaders.
red_flags: Large USD 11.75bn all-cash deal raises integration and leverage risk
Tariff one-year review clause creates uncertainty for generics post-2027
Stock has run up sharply on deal news - profit-taking risk near-term
gRevlimid cliff weighing on Q4 FY26 base business
catalysts: Organon deal closing milestones and financing details
FDA approvals on specialty pipeline (14 pending NDAs)

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results announced Apr 27: net profit up 11.15% YoY to Rs 10,839 cr; revenue up 6% to Rs 46,490 cr; EBITDA Rs 17,917 cr (+12%); EBITDA margin expanded to 39% from 36%. Final dividend Rs 5.25/share declared (FY26 total Rs 26.75). Stock closed at Rs 456.90, ~+5% post-results, +15.18% over 6 months. CONCERN: FY26 production down 1.7% YoY to 768.1 mt and dispatches down 2.4% to 744.8 mt - profit growth driven by realizations and cost control, not volume.
stocks.sector_context: PSU/coal sector benefiting from sustained domestic power demand and high government dividend yield support (5.8-6.9% across PSU pack). Coal demand from power sector firm but renewable energy transition is long-term overhang. E-auction prices range-bound and not mimicking higher global coal prices, capping realisation upside.
stocks.peer_comparison: Coal India is near-monopoly producer in India - few direct comps. Vs other PSU dividend plays (NTPC, ONGC, NMDC), CIL offers high dividend yield (~6%) and policy backing. NMDC dividend yield ~4%.
red_flags: Production AND dispatch declines YoY - core volume metrics negative
Wage hike revision from July 2026 for non-executives - opex pressure (~8% impact)
Profit growth not volume-led raises sustainability if coal prices soften
Mixed analyst views on near-term earnings visibility
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 5.25 record date pending
Continued power demand from heat wave season

stocks.recent_news: BLOWOUT Q4 FY26 results Apr 29: PAT surged 68% YoY to Rs 534 cr on lower credit costs and higher fee income. Stock rallied 12.44% intraday to fresh 52-week high of Rs 200.89. GNPA improved sharply to 3.27% (from 4.71% YoY); NNPA to 0.97% (from 1.28%); collection efficiency 98.9%. Loans +12.6% YoY to Rs 1.54L cr; deposits +10% to Rs 1.66L cr; retail deposits +17.7% (73.67% of total). CAR at 18.0% (well above 11.5% requirement). Final dividend Rs 1.50/share recommended. Brokerages raised target prices.
stocks.sector_context: Mixed Q4 for private banks: IndusInd recovered to PAT Rs 594 cr (vs loss YoY) but advances declined 8.7%; RBL Bank +22% advances growth but stock declined 5%; Axis Bank declined 5%. Microfinance/small finance bank space recovering after stress cycle. Bandhan transitioning from microfinance-heavy to diversified retail/SME bank with growing deposit franchise. Asset quality cycle inflecting positively.
stocks.peer_comparison: Bandhan's GNPA improvement (140bps to 3.27%) still elevated vs HDFC/ICICI peers but trajectory inflecting. PAT growth 68% leads listed banks for Q4. Stock at 52-wk high while many private banks consolidating. Best Q4 stock reaction among private banks (+12%) vs IndusInd +6%, RBL/Axis -5%. RoA 0.6% and RoE 4.8% still subpar vs sector leaders.
red_flags: Promoters (39.74% stake) reportedly exploring stake sale - governance/strategic uncertainty
GNPA at 3.27% still high vs HDFC/ICICI peers - MFI legacy book remains a watch
Sharp rally to 52-wk high may invite profit-booking near-term
RoA 0.6% / RoE 4.8% still below private bank leaders
catalysts: Promoter stake sale resolution could re-rate stock
Continued asset quality normalization in MFI book
